target_audience.primary_persona: Small business operators and founders using AI assistants to automate daily operations
pain_points: Fragmented tools across inbox, CRM, and task management
Slow response times and manual follow-up in lead/customer workflows
Difficulty orchestrating AI agents reliably in production
goals: Automate business operations with autonomous agents
Deploy customizable AI-driven assistants for specific business contexts
target_audience.usage_context: Used as a browser-based operations dashboard and assistant workspace during day-to-day business operations, with deployment-specific configurations for different clients/environments.

product_vision.one_liner: An agentic AI operations platform that helps businesses run and scale operations through autonomous assistants.
product_vision.problem_statement: Businesses struggle to coordinate repetitive operations across multiple systems, causing delays, inconsistency, and high manual overhead.
product_vision.value_proposition: Bitbit combines AI agent orchestration, workflow execution, and modern operational UI into a single customizable platform, reducing manual work while keeping teams in control.
success_metrics: Increase in automated task completion rate
Reduction in manual operational handling time
Deployment adoption across multiple business contexts
Improved user retention and daily active usage in assistant surfaces

existing_features: Monorepo with multiple deployable Next.js applications
Personal assistant frontend with Supabase and AI integrations
Core package for shared logic and database migration/seeding
Agent registry and role/action execution wiring
UI components for kanban, tabs, skeleton states, and notification patterns
known_gaps: High-level documentation is sparse/inconsistent at repo root
Test structure is not clearly centralized at repository level
Roadmap documentation not present in expected location
Potentially unclear service boundaries across multiple apps/modules
technical_debt: Rapid feature iteration implied by frequent refactors in recent commits
Mixed dependency/install state across workspaces (core node_modules missing locally)
TODO/FIXME visibility is noisy due to third-party/vendor files unless filtered
Monorepo complexity may require stronger conventions and architectural docs

differentiators: Domain-specific business operations focus rather than generic automation
Agentic workflow execution integrated with operational UI
Multi-deployment architecture enabling tailored client contexts
Combination of AI reasoning, notifications, and execution harness in one stack
competitive_context.market_position: Positioned as an emerging AI-native operations platform for SMBs and teams that need customizable autonomous workflows beyond simple no-code automations.

technical: Depends on external AI APIs (Anthropic) and data services (Supabase)
Frontend-heavy architecture built on Next.js workspaces
Some services rely on specific integrations (e.g., IMAP/email, Telegram, SQLite)
resources: Likely small team with fast iteration cadence
Product and engineering effort split across multiple apps/packages
Ongoing UI/UX and architecture work suggests limited bandwidth for deep stabilization
dependencies: Supabase platform reliability and schema evolution
